meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve no testing knowledge (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no testing knowledge. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Solve No Testing Knowledge with Antigravity
content.seo_description: Overcome testing gaps as a freelancer. Learn to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to deliver production-grade, bug-free apps in record time.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template eliminates the 'no testing' bottleneck by providing a pre-configured, modular architecture that automates unit and integration tests. By pairing this with Claude Code, you can generate reliable test suites instantly, ensuring your client deliverables are robust, scalable, and professional without manual QA overhead.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, a looming deadline, and a client who expects enterprise-grade reliability. The problem isn't your coding ability; it’s the paralyzing fear that your app will shatter the moment a user touches it. You lack the time to master complex testing frameworks, yet you know that shipping without tests is a ticking time bomb for your reputation.

Stop trading sleep for manual QA. The Antigravity project template acts as your architectural skeleton, enforcing best practices by default. When combined with the reasoning power of Claude Code, you aren't just writing code—you’re orchestrating a self-validating system that allows you to ship faster while maintaining the highest professional standards.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Skeleton
steps.description: Scaffold your project using the Antigravity CLI to ensure all testing dependencies and folder structures are pre-configured.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project with the Antigravity standard stack, ensuring Vitest and Playwright are configured for an E2E testing workflow.

steps.title: Define Business Logic with TDD
steps.description: Use Claude Code to create the test file before the implementation to ensure your logic meets the requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of unit tests for the auth controller that covers happy path and edge case scenarios.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Generate vitest files for auth.controller.ts based on the provided project specs.'

steps.title: Automate Implementation
steps.description: Have Claude write the code to satisfy the tests you just created, ensuring no functionality is added without coverage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the implementation for the auth controller that passes all newly created unit tests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Implement auth.controller.ts to pass the vitest suite generated in the previous step.'

steps.title: Integration Testing for API Endpoints
steps.description: Use Antigravity’s built-in mock server to verify that your API endpoints interact correctly with the database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate an integration test that mocks the database and verifies the user registration API flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Create an integration test for the registration endpoint using the Antigravity mock server.'

steps.title: Continuous Validation Loop
steps.description: Set up a file-watcher that triggers your test suite every time a save event occurs to prevent regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the project's watch mode to provide immediate feedback on failing tests.

pro_tips: Always generate 'negative' test cases first; it forces the code to handle errors gracefully before you even finish the happy path.
Use Claude Code to document your test coverage in a README.md file—clients pay more when they see a 'Quality Assurance' section.
Treat your test suite as a 'living spec' document; if a client changes a requirement, update the test first to see exactly where the code breaks.
Leverage the Antigravity 'snapshot' feature to automatically verify UI components without manual visual inspection.
Run your test suite in a GitHub Action on every push; it automates the 'professionalism' layer of your freelance business.
content.next_steps: Now that you have a testing foundation, integrate your project into a CI/CD pipeline to ensure every commit is production-ready. Start by deploying your first Antigravity project to Vercel or Railway.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing if your code works and start delivering with absolute confidence. Build faster, charge more, and never worry about breaking production again. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
